1: From nasa'; a burden; specifically, tribute, or porterage; figuratively, an utterance, chiefly a doom, especially singing; mental, desire.
Capital of Assyr. Of foreign origin; Nineveh, the capital of Assyria.
A missive, document, writing, book. Or ciphrah; from caphar; properly, writing; by implication, a book.
Vision. From chazah; a sight, i.e. A dream, revelation, or oracle.
An Israelite. prophet. From nacham; comfortable; Nachum, An Israelite. prophet.
A native of Elkosh. Patrial from a name of uncertain derivation; an Elkoshite or native of Elkosh.
10: To interweave. A primitive root; to entwine.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
Or ciyrah; or cirah; from a primitive root meaning to boil up; a pot; also a thorn; by implication, a hook.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
A drink, liquor. From caba'; potation, concretely, or abstractly.
To quaff to satiety, become tipsy. A primitive root; to quaff to satiety, i.e. Become tipsy.
Full. From male'; full or filling; also fulness; adverbially, fully.
11: From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To advise, counsel. A primitive root; to advise; reflexively, to deliberate or resolve.
Worthlessness. From bliy and ya'al; without profit, worthlessness; by extension, destruction, wickedness.
12: Thus, here. From the prefix k and huw'; properly, like this, i.e. By implication, thus; also here; or now.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
Complete, safe, at peace. From shalam; complete; especially friendly.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
To shear. A primitive root; to cut off; specifically to shear a flock or shave the hair; figuratively to destroy an enemy.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
Though I have afflicted you, O Judah,
To be bowed down or afflicted. A primitive root; to depress literally or figuratively, transitive or intransitive.
To be bowed down or afflicted. A primitive root; to depress literally or figuratively, transitive or intransitive.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
13: Now. From eth; at this time, whether adverb, conjunction or expletive.
To break, break in pieces. A primitive root; to burst.
A shaking, pole, bar (of a yoke). From mowt; a wavering, i.e. Fall; by implication, a pole; hence, a yoke.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
To pull, draw, or tear away, apart, or off. A primitive root; to tear off.
A band, bond. Also feminine mowcerah; or mocrah; from yacar; properly, chastisement, i.e. a halter; figuratively, restraint.
14: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To lay charge (upon), give charge (to), command, order. A primitive root; to constitute, enjoin.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To sow, scatter seed. A primitive root; to sow; figuratively, to disseminate, plant, fructify.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
To cut off, cut down. A primitive root; to cut; by implication, to destroy or consume; specifically, to covenant.
An idol, image. From pacal; an idol.
From nacak; properly, a pouring over, i.e. Fusion of metal; by implication, a libation, i.e. League; concretely a coverlet.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
A grave, sepulcher. Or qibrah; from qabar; a sepulchre.
To be slight, swift or trifling. A primitive root; to be light, literally Or figuratively.
15: Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
Foot. From ragal; a foot; by implication, a step; by euphem. The pudenda.
of one who brings good news,
To bear tidings. A primitive root; properly, to be fresh, i.e. Full (figuratively) cheerful); to announce.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
Or shalom; from shalam; safe, i.e. well, happy, friendly; also welfare, i.e. Health, prosperity, peace.
A primitive root; properly, to move in a circle, i.e. to march in a sacred procession, to observe a festival; by implication, to be giddy.
A festival gathering, feast, pilgrim feast. Or chag; from chagag; a festival, or a victim therefor.
From yadah; celebrated; Jehudah, the name of five Israelites; also of the tribe descended from the first, and of its territory.
A primitive root; to be safe; figuratively, to be completed; by implication, to be friendly; by extension, to reciprocate.
A vow. Or neder; from nadar; a promise; also a thing promised.
Worthlessness. From bliy and ya'al; without profit, worthlessness; by extension, destruction, wickedness.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To add. A primitive root; to add or augment.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
To cut off, cut down. A primitive root; to cut; by implication, to destroy or consume; specifically, to covenant.
2: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Jealous. For qanna'; jealous or angry.
To avenge, take vengeance. A primitive root; to grudge, i.e. Avenge or punish.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To avenge, take vengeance. A primitive root; to grudge, i.e. Avenge or punish.
Owner, lord. From ba'al; a master; hence, a husband, or owner.
Heat, rage. Or chemac; from yacham; heat; figuratively, anger, poison.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To avenge, take vengeance. A primitive root; to grudge, i.e. Avenge or punish.
Narrow, a tight place, a pebble, an opponent. Or tsar; from tsarar; narrow; a tight place; also a pebble; an opponent.
To keep. A primitive root; to guard; figuratively, to cherish.
Hating, an adversary. Or owyeb; active participle of 'ayab; hating; an adversary.
3: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
Great. Or gadol; from gadal; great; hence, older; also insolent.
Or kowach; from an unused root meaning to be firm; vigor, literally or figuratively; also a large lizard.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be empty or clean. A primitive root; to be clean; by implication to be bare, i.e. Extirpated.
To be empty or clean. A primitive root; to be clean; by implication to be bare, i.e. Extirpated.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
A storm wind. From cuwph; a hurricane.
A storm. Feminine of sa'ar; a hurricane.
A cloud mass, cloud. From anan; a cloud, i.e. The nimbus or thunder-cloud.
Dust. From root of 'abaq; light particles.
Foot. From ragal; a foot; by implication, a step; by euphem. The pudenda.
4: To rebuke. A primitive root; to chide.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
To be dry, dried up, or withered. A primitive root; to be ashamed, confused or disappointed; also to dry up or wither.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
A stream, river. From nahar; a stream; figuratively, prosperity.
To parch, to desolate, destroy, kill. Or chareb; a primitive root; to parch i.e. to desolate, destroy, kill.
Smooth, a region E. of the Jordan. Of uncertain derivation; Bashan, a region East of the Jordan.
The same as karmel; Karmel, the name of a hill and of a town in Palestine.
To be weak, languish. A primitive root; to droop; by implication to be sick, to mourn.
A bud, sprout. From parach; a calyx; generally, bloom.
A wooded mountain range on the N. border of Isr. From lbab; white mountain; Lebanon, a mountain range in Palestine.
To be weak, languish. A primitive root; to droop; by implication to be sick, to mourn.
5: Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
To quake, shake. A primitive root; to undulate, partic. Through fear; specifically, to spring.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
A hill. Feminine from the same as Geba'; a hillock.
To melt. A primitive root; to melt, i.e. Literally, or figuratively.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
From yabal; the earth; by extension, the globe; by implication, its inhabitants; specifically, a partic. Land, as Babylonia, Palestine.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
6: An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.
Indignation. From za'am; strictly froth at the mouth, i.e. fury.
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
(burning of) anger. Or charon; from charah; a burning of anger.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
Heat, rage. Or chemac; from yacham; heat; figuratively, anger, poison.
To pour forth, be poured out. A primitive root; to flow forth; by implication, to liquify.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.
Rock, cliff. Or tsur; from tsuwr; properly, a cliff; generally, a rock or boulder; figuratively, a refuge; also an edge.
To pull down, break down. A primitive root; to tear down.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
7: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
(also mauwz); or mahoz (also ma, uz; from azaz; a fortified place; figuratively, a defence.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Tightness, a female rival. Feminine of tsar; tightness; transitively, a female rival.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
To seek refuge. A primitive root; to flee for protection; figuratively, to confide in.
8: To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
A flood. Or sheteph; from shataph; a deluge.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Completion, complete destruction, consumption, annihilation. From kalah; a completion; adverb, completely; also destruction.
Or maqom; also mqowmah; or mqomah; from quwm; properly, a standing, i.e. A spot; but used widely of a locality; also of a condition.
To pursue, chase, persecute. A primitive root; to run after (of time) gone by).
Hating, an adversary. Or owyeb; active participle of 'ayab; hating; an adversary.
From chashak; the dark; hence darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness.
9: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Completion, complete destruction, consumption, annihilation. From kalah; a completion; adverb, completely; also destruction.
Tightness, a female rival. Feminine of tsar; tightness; transitively, a female rival.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
A beat, foot, anvil, occurrence. Or pa;amah; from pa'am; a stroke, literally or figuratively.

1: To be dispersed or scattered. A primitive root; to dash in pieces, literally or figuratively.
A primitive root; to ascend, intransitively or actively; used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
To watch, guard, keep. A primitive root; to guard, in a good sense Or a bad one.
Siegeworks, rampart. Or mtsurah; feminine of matsowr; a hemming in, i.e. a mound, or a rampart, fortification.
A primitive root; properly, to lean forward, i.e. To peer into the distance; by implication, to observe, await.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
A primitive root; to fasten upon; hence, to seize, be strong, obstinate; to bind, restrain, conquer.
Loins. From an unused root meaning to be slender; properly, the waist or small of the back; only in plural the loins.
To be stout, strong, bold, alert. A primitive root; to be alert, physically or mentally.
Muchness, force, abundance. From the same as 'uwd; properly, vehemence, i.e. vehemently; by implication, wholly, speedily, etc.
Or kowach; from an unused root meaning to be firm; vigor, literally or figuratively; also a large lizard.
10: Emptiness. Feminine passive participle of an unused root; emptiness.
Emptiness. From the same as buwqah; emptiness.
To waste, lay waste. A primitive root; to annihilate.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
To dissolve, melt. A primitive root; to liquefy; figuratively, to waste, to faint.
The knee. From barak; a knee.
Tottering, staggering. From puwq; a tottering.
Loins. From an unused root meaning to be slender; properly, the waist or small of the back; only in plural the loins.
Anguish. Feminine from the same as Chalchuwl; writhing; by implication, terror.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
To gather, collect. A primitive root; to grasp, i.e. Collect.
Perhaps beauty. From pa'ar; properly, illuminated, i.e. A glow; as noun, a flush.
11: A lion. Or earyeh; from 'arah; a lion.
Dwelling, habitation. Or maiyn; from the same as ownah; an abode, of God, men or animals; hence, a retreat.
A pasturage, pasture. From ra'ah in the sense of feeding; pasture; also the haunt of wild animals.
Young lion. From kaphar; a village; also a young lion.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
A lion. Or earyeh; from 'arah; a lion.
There, thither. A primitive particle; there then; often thither, or thence.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
A lion. Or earyeh; from 'arah; a lion.
A whelp. Or gur; perhaps from guwr; a cub, especially of the lion.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
To tremble, be terrified. A primitive root; to shudder with terror; hence, to fear; also to hasten.
12: A lion. Or earyeh; from 'arah; a lion.
To tear, rend, pluck. A primitive root; to pluck off or pull to pieces; causatively to supply with food.
Sufficiency, enough. Of uncertain derivation; enough, used chiefly with preposition in phrases.
A whelp. Or gorah; a variation of guwr.
To strangle. A primitive root; to be narrow; by implication, to throttle, or To choke oneself to death.
To be full, to fill. Or malae; a primitive root, to fill or be full of, in a wide application.
A hole. Or chor; the same as chuwr; a cavity, socket, den.
Prey, food, a leaf. From taraph; something torn, i.e. A fragment, e.g. A fresh leaf, prey, food.
Habitation. Or msonah; feminine of ma'own, and meaning the same.
Torn animal, torn flesh. Feminine of tereph; prey, i.e. Flocks devoured by animals.
13: Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Or tsbadah; from tsaba'; a mass of persons, especially reg. Organized for war; by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively.
From rakab; a vehicle; by implication, a team; by extension, cavalry; by analogy a rider, i.e. The upper millstone.
To kindle, consume, to be, brutish. A primitive root; to kindle, i.e. Consume; also to be(-come) brutish.
Smoke. From ashan; smoke, literally or figuratively.
A sword. From charab; drought; also a cutting instrument, as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
Young lion. From kaphar; a village; also a young lion.
To cut off, cut down. A primitive root; to cut; by implication, to destroy or consume; specifically, to covenant.
Prey, food, a leaf. From taraph; something torn, i.e. A fragment, e.g. A fresh leaf, prey, food.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
A messenger. From an unused root meaning to despatch as a deputy; a messenger; specifically, of God, i.e. An angel.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
2: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
Arrogance, majesty, ornament. From ga'ah; the same as ga'avah.
A son of Isaac, also his desc. From aqab; heel-catcher; Jaakob, the Israelitish patriarch.
Arrogance, majesty, ornament. From ga'ah; the same as ga'avah.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
A primitive root; to pour out, i.e. To empty, figuratively, to depopulate; by analogy, to spread out.
A primitive root; to pour out, i.e. To empty, figuratively, to depopulate; by analogy, to spread out.
Perhaps to go to ruin. A primitive root; to decay, i.e. ruin.
Branch, twig, shoot. Or zmorah; and zmor; from zamar; a twig.
3: A shield. Also feminine mginnah; from ganan; a shield; figuratively, a protector; also the scaly hide of the crocodile.
Strong, mighty. Or gibbor; intensive from the same as geber; powerful; by implication, warrior, tyrant.
To be red. To show blood, i.e. Flush or turn rosy.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
From chuwl; probably a force, whether of men, means or other resources; an army, wealth, virtue, valor, strength.
To clothe in scarlet. A denominative from towla'; to crimson, i.e. Dye that color.
Probably iron, steel. From an unused root meaning to divide; a cleaver, i.e. Iron armature.
From rakab; a vehicle; by implication, a team; by extension, cavalry; by analogy a rider, i.e. The upper millstone.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
Cypress or fir. Of uncertain derivation; a cypress Tree; hence, a lance or a musical instrument.
To quiver, shake, reel. A primitive root; to reel, i.e. to brandish.
4: From rakab; a vehicle; by implication, a team; by extension, cavalry; by analogy a rider, i.e. The upper millstone.
The outside, a street. Or chuts; from an unused root meaning to sever; properly, separate by a wall, i.e. Outside, outdoors.
To run, run about, rush. A primitive root; to course; by implication, to seek greedily.
A broad open place, plaza. Or rchowb; from rachab; a width, i.e. avenue or area.
Sight, appearance, vision. From ra'ah; a view; also an appearance, whether a shape, or a vision.
A torch. Or lappid; from an unused root probably meaning to shine; a flambeau, lamp or flame.
To run. A primitive root; to run.
Lightning. From baraq; lightning; by analogy, a gleam; concretely, a flashing sword.
5: Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.
Majestic. From 'adar; wide or large; figuratively, powerful.
To stumble, stagger, totter. A primitive root; to totter or waver; by implication, to falter, stumble, faint or fall.
A going, way, traveling company. Feminine of haliyk; a walking; by implication, a procession or march, a caravan.
To hasten. A primitive root; properly, to be liquid or flow easily, i.e. to hurry; often used adverbially, promptly.
A wall. Feminine active participle of an unused root apparently meaning to join; a wall of protection.
Or sakak; a primitive root; properly, to entwine as a screen; by implication, to fence in, cover over, protect.
A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
6: A stream, river. From nahar; a stream; figuratively, prosperity.
A gate. From sha'ar in its original sense; an opening, i.e. Door or gate.
To open wide, to loosen, begin, plough, carve. A primitive root; to open wide; specifically, to loosen, begin, plough, carve.
A palace, temple. Probably from yakol; a large public building, such as a palace or temple.
To melt. A primitive root; to melt, i.e. Literally, or figuratively.
7: To take one's stand, stand. A prim root; to station, in various applications.
To uncover, remove. A primitive root; to denude; by implication, to exile; figuratively, to reveal.
A primitive root; to ascend, intransitively or actively; used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative.
A maid, handmaid. Apparently a primitive word; a maid-servant or female slave.
A primitive root; to drive forth, i.e. Lead, carry away; reflexively, to proceed; also, to sigh.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
Dove. Probably from the same as yayin; a dove.
To sound the timbrel, beat. A primitive root; to drum, i.e. Play on the tambourine.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
8: Capital of Assyr. Of foreign origin; Nineveh, the capital of Assyria.
A pool, pond. From barak; a reservoir.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
They. Or hemmah; masculine plural from halak; they.
To flee, escape. A primitive root; to flit, i.e. Vanish away.
To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.
To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
To turn. A primitive root; to turn; by implication, to face, i.e. Appear, look, etc.
9: To spoil, plunder. A primitive root; to plunder.
Silver, money. From kacaph; silver; by implication, money.
To spoil, plunder. A primitive root; to plunder.
Gold. From an unused root meaning to shimmer; gold, figuratively, something gold-colored, as oil, a clear sky.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
End, extremity. Or qetseh; from qatsah; an extremity.
Adjustment, structure, equipage. Feminine passive participle of takan; adjustment, i.e. Structure; by implication, equipage.
Rarely kabod; from kabad; properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Desire, delight. Feminine of chemed; delight.
An article, utensil, vessel. From kalah; something prepared, i.e. Any apparatus.

1: Ah! alas! ha! A prolonged form of how; oh!
Excitement. Or par; or ayar; from uwr a city in the widest sense.
Blood. From damam; blood of man or an animal; by analogy, the juice of the grape; figuratively bloodshed.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Lying, leanness. From kachash; literally, a failure of flesh, i.e. Emaciation; figuratively, hypocrisy.
Full. From male'; full or filling; also fulness; adverbially, fully.
Parting of ways, plunder. From paraq; rapine; also a fork.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To depart, remove. A primitive root; to withdraw.
Prey, food, a leaf. From taraph; something torn, i.e. A fragment, e.g. A fresh leaf, prey, food.
10: Exiles, exile. Or golah; active participle feminine of galah; exile; concretely and collectively exiles.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
Exiled, captured, exile, booty. From shbuw; exiled; captured; as noun, exile; by extension, booty.
A child. Or lolal; from uwl; a suckling.
To dash in pieces. A primitive root; to dash down.
Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
The outside, a street. Or chuts; from an unused root meaning to sever; properly, separate by a wall, i.e. Outside, outdoors.
To cast a lot. A primitive root; properly, to handle, i.e. To throw, e.g. Lots.
Or goral; from an unused root meaning to be rough; properly, a pebble, i.e. A lot; figuratively, a portion or destiny.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Or kabed; a primitive root; to be heavy, i.e. In a bad sense or in a good sense (in the same two senses).
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Great. Or gadol; from gadal; great; hence, older; also insolent.
To bind. A primitive root; to fasten.
(Isa. 'Abiyshuwae:; and ziq; or zeq; from zanaq; properly, what leaps forth, i.e. Flash of fire, or a burning arrow; also a bond.
11: A primitive root; to become tipsy; in a qualified sense, to satiate with a stimulating drink or influence.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
To conceal. A primitive root; to veil from sight, i.e. Conceal.
To seek. A primitive root; to search out; by implication, to strive after.
(also mauwz); or mahoz (also ma, uz; from azaz; a fortified place; figuratively, a defence.
Hating, an adversary. Or owyeb; active participle of 'ayab; hating; an adversary.
12: The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Fortification. Also feminine mibtsarah; from batsar; a fortification, castle, or fortified city; figuratively, a defender.
Fig tree. Or t:enah; perhaps of foreign derivation; the fig.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
First fruits. From bakar; the first-fruits of the crop.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
A primitive root; to waver, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively.
To fall, lie. A primitive root; to fall, in a great variety of applications.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
13: Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
Woman, wife, female. Feminine of 'iysh or 'enowsh; irregular plural, nashiym; a woman.
Inward part, midst. From qarab; properly, the nearest part, i.e. The center, whether literal, figurative or adverbial.
A gate. From sha'ar in its original sense; an opening, i.e. Door or gate.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
To open wide, to loosen, begin, plough, carve. A primitive root; to open wide; specifically, to loosen, begin, plough, carve.
To open wide, to loosen, begin, plough, carve. A primitive root; to open wide; specifically, to loosen, begin, plough, carve.
Hating, an adversary. Or owyeb; active participle of 'ayab; hating; an adversary.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
A bar. From barach; a bolt.
14: To draw (water). A primitive root; to bale up water.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
Siege enclosure, siege, entrenchment. Or matsuwr; from tsuwr; something hemming in, i.e. a mound, a siege, distress; or a fastness.
A primitive root; to fasten upon; hence, to seize, be strong, obstinate; to bind, restrain, conquer.
Fortification. Also feminine mibtsarah; from batsar; a fortification, castle, or fortified city; figuratively, a defender.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Mud, mire, clay. From an unused root meaning apparently to be sticky; mud or clay; figuratively, calamity.
To trample. A primitive root; to tread upon.
From chamar; properly, a bubbling up, i.e. Of water, a wave; of earth, mire or clay; also a heap; hence, a chomer or dry measure.
A primitive root; to fasten upon; hence, to seize, be strong, obstinate; to bind, restrain, conquer.
A brick mold, quadrangle. From laban; a brick-kiln.
15: There, thither. A primitive particle; there then; often thither, or thence.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
A sword. From charab; drought; also a cutting instrument, as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement.
To cut off, cut down. A primitive root; to cut; by implication, to destroy or consume; specifically, to covenant.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
(a kind of) locust. From an unused root meaning to lick up; a devourer; specifically, the young locust.
Or kabed; a primitive root; to be heavy, i.e. In a bad sense or in a good sense (in the same two senses).
(a kind of) locust. From an unused root meaning to lick up; a devourer; specifically, the young locust.
Or kabed; a primitive root; to be heavy, i.e. In a bad sense or in a good sense (in the same two senses).
(a kind of) locust. From rabah; a locust.
16: To be or become much, many or great. A primitive root; to increase.
Probably to go about. A primitive root; to travel for trading.
A star. Probably from the same as Kabbown or kavah; a star; figuratively, a prince.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
(a kind of) locust. From an unused root meaning to lick up; a devourer; specifically, the young locust.
To strip off, make a dash, raid. A primitive root; to spread out; by analogy, to strip.
To fly, to faint. A primitive root; to cover; hence to fly; also to faint.
17: Perhaps consecrated ones, princes. From nazar; a prince.
(a kind of) locust. From rabah; a locust.
A scribe, marshal. Of foreign derivation; a military governor.
A primitive root; properly, to incline; by implication, to decline; specifically, to pitch a tent; gen. To encamp.
A wall. Feminine of gader; enclosure.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
From an unused root meaning to be brilliant; the sun; by implication, the east; figuratively, a ray, i.e. A notched battlement.
To rise, come forth. A primitive root; properly, to irradiate, i.e. To rise; specifically, to appear.
A primitive root; properly, to wave to and fro; figuratively, to rove, flee, or to drive away.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Or maqom; also mqowmah; or mqomah; from quwm; properly, a standing, i.e. A spot; but used widely of a locality; also of a condition.
Where?. Perhaps from 'aiyn; where? Hence how?.
18: Ashshur, the second son of Shem; also his descendants and the country occupied by them, its region and its empire.
A primitive root; to tend a flock; i.e. Pasture it; intransitively, to graze; generally to rule; by extension, to associate with.
To be drowsy, slumber. A primitive root; to slumber.
Majestic. From 'adar; wide or large; figuratively, powerful.
A primitive root (by transmission) to shakab through the idea of lodging; compare cikluwth, shakam); to reside or permanently stay.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
Probably to spring about. A primitive root; to spread; figuratively, act proudly.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
To gather, collect. A primitive root; to grasp, i.e. Collect.
19: As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
Lessening, alleviation. Feminine of keheh; properly, a weakening; figuratively, alleviation, i.e. Cure.
Or sheber; from shabar; a fracture, figuratively, ruin; specifically, a solution.
A blow, wound, slaughter. Or makkeh; from nakah; a blow; by implication, a wound; figuratively, carnage, also pestilence.
A primitive root; properly, to be rubbed or worn; hence to be weak, sick, afflicted; or to grieve, make sick; also to stroke, entreat.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
A hearing, report. From shama'; something heard, i.e. A sound, rumor, announcement; abstractly, audience.
A primitive root; to clatter, i.e. Slap, clang; by analogy, to drive; by implication, to become bondsman by handclasping).
Hollow or flat of the hand, palm, sole (of the foot), a pan. From kaphaph; the hollow hand or palm; figuratively, power.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
From an unused root meaning to stretch; properly, continuance; but used only constant; ellipt. The regular sacrifice.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
2: Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
A scourge, whip. From shuwt; a lash.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
A quaking, shaking. From ra'ash; vibration, bounding, uproar.
A wheel. Or pophan; from an unused root meaning to revolve; a wheel.
To rush, dash (of a horse). A primitive root; to curvet or move irregularly.
A swallow, swift (type of bird). Or cuc; from an unused root meaning to skip; a horse; also a swallow.
To skip about. A primitive root; properly, to stamp, i.e. To spring about.
A chariot. Feminine of merkab; a chariot.
3: A primitive root; to ascend, intransitively or actively; used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative.
A steed, a driver, cavalry. From parash; a steed (compare cuwc); also a driver, i.e. cavalry.
Flame, blade. From an usused root meaning to gleam; a flash; figuratively, a sharply polished blade or point of a weapon.
A sword. From charab; drought; also a cutting instrument, as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement.
Lightning. From baraq; lightning; by analogy, a gleam; concretely, a flashing sword.
A spear. From chanah; a lance.
Multitude, abundance, greatness. From rabab; abundance.
Pierced, polluted. From chalal; pierced; figuratively, polluted.
Heaviness, mass. From kabad; weight, multitude, vehemence.
Corpse, carcass. From pagar; a carcase, whether of man or beast; figuratively, an idolatrous image.
A body, corpse. Prolonged for gevah; a body, whether alive or dead.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
End, extremity. Or qetseh; from qatsah; an extremity.
To stumble, stagger, totter. A primitive root; to totter or waver; by implication, to falter, stumble, faint or fall.
A body, corpse. Prolonged for gevah; a body, whether alive or dead.
4: Multitude, abundance, greatness. From rabab; abundance.
Fornication. From zanah; adultery; figuratively, idolatry.
To commit fornication, be a harlot. A primitive root; to commit adultery; figuratively, to commit idolatry.
Favor, grace. From chanan; graciousness, i.e. Subjective or objective.
A mistress. Feminine of ba'al; a mistress.
To sell. A primitive root; to sell, literally, or figuratively.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
Fornication. From zanah; adultery; figuratively, idolatry.
From shaphah; a family, i.e. Circle of relatives; figuratively, a class, a species or sort; by extens. A tribe or people.
5: Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Or tsbadah; from tsaba'; a mass of persons, especially reg. Organized for war; by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively.
To uncover, remove. A primitive root; to denude; by implication, to exile; figuratively, to reveal.
Skirt (of a robe). From an unused root meaning to hang down; a skirt; by implication, a bottom edge.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
A bare or naked place. From arah; a nude place, i.e. the pudenda, or a vacant space.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
Ignominy, dishonor. From qalah; disgrace; the pudenda.
Kingdom, sovereignty, dominion, reign. From malak; dominion, i.e. the estate or the country.
6: To throw, fling, cast. A primitive root; to throw out, down or away.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Detested thing. Or shiqquts; from shaqats; disgusting, i.e. Filthy; especially idolatrous or an idol.
A primitive root; to wilt; generally, to fall away, fail, faint; figuratively, to be foolish or wicked; causatively, to despise, disgrace.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Looking, seeing, sight. From ra'ah; sight, whether abstractly or concretely.
7: To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
A primitive root; properly, to wave to and fro; figuratively, to rove, flee, or to drive away.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Capital of Assyr. Of foreign origin; Nineveh, the capital of Assyria.
A primitive root; properly, to be burly, i.e. powerful; by implication, to ravage.
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
A primitive root; to nod, i.e. Waver; figuratively, to wander, flee, disappear; also, to console, deplore, or taunt.
Whence?. Probably identical with 'ayin in the sense of query.
To seek. A primitive root; to search out; by implication, to strive after.
A primitive root; properly, to sigh, i.e. Breathe strongly; by implication, to be sorry, i.e. to pity, console or rue; or to avenge.
8: To be good, well, glad, or pleasing. A primitive root; to be make well, literally or figuratively.
An Egyptian city. Of Egyptian origin; No, the capital of Upper Egypt.
An Egyptian god. Of Egyptian derivation; Amon, a deity of Egypt.
A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
Circuit, round about. Or cbiybah; from cabab; a circle, neighbour, or environs; but chiefly around.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
Rampart, fortress. Or chel; a collateral form of chayil; an army; also an intrenchment.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
A wall. Feminine active participle of an unused root apparently meaning to join; a wall of protection.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
9: Probably of foreign origin; Cush, the name of a son of Ham, and of his territory; also of an Israelite.
A son of Ham, also his desc. and their country in N.W. Africa. Dual of matsowr; Mitsrajim, i.e. Upper and Lower Egypt.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
End, extremity. Or qetseh; from qatsah; an extremity.
Might. Feminine of otsem; powerfulness; by extension, numerousness.
Of foreign origin; Put, a son of Ham, also the name of his descendants or their region, and of a Persian tribe.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Help, helper, assistance. Or mezrath; feminine of ezer; aid.
